findings.evidence: `rg` shows existing `private theorem trace_eq_zero_of_sq_algebraMap_of_not_mem_range` at `code/TauCeti/FieldTheory/Trace.lean:32` and public `NumberField.trace_eq_zero_of_sq_ratCast_of_not_mem_range` at `:65`; roadmap only names `trace_eq_zero_of_sq_ratCast` at `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/EffectiveBounds/README.md:98`.
findings.fix: Import/reuse `TauCeti.FieldTheory.Trace`; keep the general helper private here, or deliberately promote the existing helper in `FieldTheory/Trace.lean` instead of adding a second public API in `EffectiveBounds`.
findings.issue: `TauCeti.Algebra.trace_eq_zero_of_sq_mem_range` publicly promotes the general finite-extension helper even though the named downstream target only needs the number-field rational form, and the same general proof already exists privately in `TauCeti/FieldTheory/Trace.lean`.

summary: The trace-form discriminant API is useful, but the PR exposes duplicate/general trace-zero surface beyond the roadmap target instead of reusing the existing trace API.
